title: Wylia hispanica
intro:
I couldn't find any information about a plant called "Wylia hispanica." However, I found a plant called "Wild Yam" or "Dioscorea villosa" is often referred to as Wyliea, but it seems you are referring to "Wyliea" which I believe is an old name for Dioscorea villosa,  or possibly Dioscorea hispanica.

If you are referring to Dioscorea hispanica, here is a paragraph about it:

Dioscorea hispanica, commonly known as Spanish wild yam, is a plant species in the Dioscoreaceae family, characterized by its edible tubers and medicinal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including pain relief, menopausal symptoms, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dioscorea hispanica include dioscoretin, diosgenin, and other saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Dioscorea hispanica is often used in herbal preparations such as capsules, tinctures, and teas to support women's health and alleviate menopausal symptoms.
